sessions.title: Build Beautiful Cross-Platform Apps with Uno Platform
sessions.abstract: Learn how Uno Platform lets you build beautiful, native, single-codebase apps for Windows, the web, mobile, and desktop. We'll cover the Live Wizard, Hot Design, Figma-to-XAML, C# Markup, the Uno MCP servers, and migrating WinUI apps to cross-platform.

sessions.title: Modernizing WinUI Apps with .NET 10
sessions.abstract: Take your existing WinUI 3 / Windows App SDK apps to the next level with .NET 10 features, performance improvements, and cross-platform reach via Uno Platform.

sessions.title: MVUX Deep Dive: Reactive State for .NET Apps
sessions.abstract: MVUX combines the best of MVVM and Elm-style architectures. Learn how immutable records, feeds, and states create predictable, testable apps.

sessions.title: From Figma to Production with Uno Platform Studio
sessions.abstract: Bridge the design-developer gap. Export Figma frames directly to XAML or C# Markup, and iterate visually with Hot Design.

sessions.title: AI-Assisted Cross-Platform Development with MCP
sessions.abstract: Pair your AI agent with the Uno MCP servers for grounded code generation and live app inspection. We'll build a feature end-to-end with Copilot driving.

speakers.name: Alvin Ashcraft
speakers.title: Sr. Content Developer, Microsoft Learn
speakers.bio: Alvin Ashcraft is a Microsoft MVP alumnus and the curator of the Morning Dew, a daily collection of links for .NET developers. He has spent over 25 years building Windows and web apps and writes about .NET, Uno Platform, and developer productivity.

speakers.name: Jane Mueller
speakers.title: Principal Engineer, Contoso
speakers.bio: Jane has been shipping desktop apps on Windows since the WinForms era. She specializes in modernizing legacy line-of-business apps with WinUI and Uno Platform.

speakers.name: Kai Schmidt
speakers.title: Independent Consultant
speakers.bio: Kai helps teams adopt reactive architectures and AI-driven workflows. He is a frequent speaker at .NET conferences across Europe.
